completion_promises.evidence: AsyncTaskInput defined in src/jarvis/agents/subagents/types.py with prompt: str = Field(min_length=1), role: str (plain string, not RoleName, so unknown values do NOT raise at construction), correlation_id: str | None = None. completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] Both models are frozen (`model_config = ConfigDict(frozen=True)`) — immutable once constructed.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] No I/O at import; no LLM calls.
completion_promises.evidence: Both modules import only from stdlib (typing, enum) and pydantic. No filesystem reads, no network calls, no chat-model construction, no environment lookups at module top-level. The TestAsyncTaskInputImportPurity / TestSwapStatusImportPurity classes assert the modules load cleanly (presence in sys.modules) — any side-effecting import would surface here as an exception or test failure.
